title: Academic conference panel
explanation:
Academic conference panel

Listen to part of a panel on education policy.

Transcript

Moderator: Our final question: can standardised testing improve equity?
Speaker A: Only if results are used to support schools, not punish them.
Speaker B: I would argue that excessive testing narrows the curriculum and increases anxiety.
Speaker A: Agreed, but abandoning measurement altogether is not realistic either.
Moderator: So the challenge is designing assessment that informs teaching without distorting it.

questions.text: Speaker A says results should [*support schools|punish schools|replace teachers].
Speaker B believes too much testing [*narrows the curriculum|improves creativity|reduces anxiety].
Speaker A thinks abandoning measurement is [*not realistic|essential|already happening].
The moderator concludes the challenge is assessment that [*informs teaching|eliminates homework|reduces funding].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The panel discusses standardised ____. | *testing | cooking | driving
Speaker B mentions increased student ____.  | *anxiety | salary | traffic
The tone is largely ____ despite disagreement. | *constructive | hostile | comic
The issue is how to design better ____.  | *assessment | furniture | weather
Speaker B uses hedging with [*I would argue|I definitely prove|Everyone knows].
The panel would interest [*policymakers and educators|only athletes|only tourists].
The final message rejects both extremes: punishment and [*no measurement|more punishment|less teaching].
